tagline: From the floor to Restaurant Manager — two promotions, ten years.

start.title: Server / Tea Boy
start.note: You are here — an entry food & beverage service role, ~0 years of experience. You take orders, serve guests, and keep the floor running.

rungs.title: Shift Supervisor / Head Server
rungs.scope: Run a shift or section end-to-end — the floor, the team on it, and the service standard — instead of working your own tables.
rungs.experience: ~3–5 years on the floor, with food-safety and beverage certification. You own a shift and the guests' experience on it.
rungs.evaluated: Evidence you already run a shift: standards you hold, servers you trained, and a service metric you moved — plus a manager who trusts you with the floor.

rungs.title: Food & Beverage / Restaurant Manager
rungs.scope: Run the whole outlet — its team, its standards, and its P&L — so every shift and every server performs, not just the one you're on.
rungs.experience: ~8–12+ years, with a hospitality-management qualification. A promotion gated on running a profitable, well-run venue, not tenure.
rungs.evaluated: Sustained venue-level impact: a P&L you own, a team you built and retained, and service standards that hold across every shift — attested by ownership.
